mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal zero lower bound monetary accommodation. This extreme easing compresses equity risk premiums and reduces discount rates on future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of April 2015 indicates sustained zero-rate regime
regime_hint: post-crisis zero lower bound monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 200,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer income. This sustained demand driver produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprrice growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141,367 thousand as of April 2015 reflects post-recovery labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline more than 10% month-over-month, signaling deterioration in residential investment demand and forward-looking economic weakness. This negative demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice growth and earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 926 thousand units as of March 2015 reflects post-recovery housing recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in recession periods (NBER-dated).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO declines month-over-month while unemployment rate UNRATE rises simultaneously, indicating stagflationary pressure: supply contraction without demand support. This dual signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings and inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly decline > 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.18 and UNRATE at 5.4% as of April 2015 show stable supply and labor conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales RSAFS and housing starts HOUST both accelerate month-over-month simultaneously, signaling broad-based demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors. This dual demand strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth > 0.4% AND HOUST monthly growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 436,772 million and HOUST at 926 thousand as of March-April 2015
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect explains less than 5% of incremental variance beyond single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate FEDFUNDS remains below 0.50%, indicating monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation. This real rate compression produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate future policy tightening and rising discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 235.74 (March 2015) and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% (April 2015) indicate low inflation and accommodative rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the real rate signal fails to predict negative returns in periods of actual policy tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate UNRATE falls below 5.0% while payroll employment PAYEMS growth remains above 150,000 persons monthly, signaling tight labor market conditions. This labor market tightness produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors interpret tight labor as evidence of durable economic expansion and wage-driven demand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% and PAYEMS at 141,367 thousand as of April 2015 show labor market near full employment
regime_hint: late-cycle tight labor market with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in documented tight labor market periods (NBER-dated).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling sustained supply-side weakness and forward-looking production contraction. This persistent supply deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings downward and anticipate recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines in 2 consecutive months with cumulative decline > 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.18 as of April 2015 reflects stable industrial conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in NBER-dated recession periods.
